sections.heading: Surgery
sections.content:
Surgery is the only potentially curative treatment for resectable BTCs. Complete resection with negative margins (R0) is the goal.

Principles:
- Resectability must be assessed by high-quality cross-sectional imaging and MDT review.
- Preoperative biopsy is not always necessary if clinical suspicion is high, but should be performed if chemotherapy is anticipated.
- Diagnostic laparoscopy should be considered to rule out occult metastatic disease, especially for gallbladder cancer and iCCA.
- Regional lymphadenectomy is recommended for accurate staging (porta hepatis for hilar, head of pancreas area for distal).
- Minimally invasive approaches are safe and effective for well-selected cases by experienced surgeons.
- Resection of the bile duct is only indicated to obtain negative margins, not routinely.
- Port site resection is not recommended as it does not improve outcomes.

Resectability criteria:
Resectability is defined by the ability to achieve an R0 resection while maintaining adequate liver function (future liver remnant ≥30-40%). Contraindications to resection include: distant metastatic disease (liver, peritoneum), lymph node metastases beyond the regional basin (celiac axis, aortocaval groove for gallbladder), extensive vascular involvement precluding safe resection, and contralateral biliary/vascular involvement (for hilar CCA). For hilar CCA, the Blumgart staging system may predict resectability.

Pre-operative optimisation:
Preoperative biliary drainage should be considered for patients with jaundice prior to resection and systemic therapy, especially for hilar CCA with a small future liver remnant. Portal vein embolization may be considered to hypertrophy the future liver remnant. Nutritional optimization and correction of coagulopathy are essential.

cards.name: Cholecystectomy + en bloc hepatic resection (segments IVB and V) + lymphadenectomy
cards.detail:
Approach: Open or minimally invasive
Indications: Gallbladder cancer
Key complications: Bile leak, bleeding, liver failure
Operative mortality: Varies by center; generally <5% in high-volume centers

cards.name: Major hepatectomy (right or left) with caudate lobectomy + hilar lymphadenectomy ± portal vein resection
cards.detail:
Approach: Open
Indications: Hilar cholangiocarcinoma
Key complications: Liver failure, biliary leak, vascular injury
Operative mortality: Higher in high-volume centers (5-10%)

cards.name: Pancreaticoduodenectomy (Whipple procedure)
cards.detail:
Approach: Open
Indications: Distal cholangiocarcinoma
Key complications: Pancreatic leak, delayed gastric emptying, hemorrhage
Operative mortality: 3-5% in high-volume centers

cards.name: Hepatic resection for iCCA
cards.detail:
Approach: Open or minimally invasive
Indications: Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma
Key complications: Bile leak, bleeding, liver failure
Operative mortality: <5%
